qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unauthenticated SSRF via Chromium URL conversion route allows internal network access
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding concerns unrestricted Chromium URL navigation and regex-only URL allow/deny filtering for SSRF; it does not identify the advisory's extraHttpHeaders scope regex compilation without a regexp2 MatchTimeout.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unauthenticated SSRF via downloadFrom feature allows arbitrary URL fetching with custom headers
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding concerns the downloadFrom HTTP fetch path and default empty URL allow/deny lists for SSRF; it does not identify the advisory's extraHttpHeaders scope regex compilation without a regexp2 MatchTimeout.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described ReDoS root cause: user-supplied extraHttpHeaders scope patterns compiled with dlclark/regexp2 in Gotenberg's Chromium routes without setting MatchTimeout. The two qualifying findings are separate SSRF issues involving URL fetching and regex-only URL filtering.
evidence: The advisory describes CWE-1333 ReDoS in gotenberg/pkg/modules/chromium/routes.go:200 where extraHttpHeaders scope patterns are compiled by dlclark/regexp2 without a timeout.
ssrf-chromium-url-route reports unrestricted Chromium URL conversion and missing private-IP or scheme validation, with impact through internal HTTP content rendered as PDF.
ssrf-download-from reports unrestricted downloadFrom server-side HTTP fetching with custom headers, with impact through internal HTTP requests and processed downloaded content.
Session metadata lists only ssrf-chromium-url-route and ssrf-download-from as final qualifying vulnerability folders.
